---
title: Research
format: html
editor: visual
---

### Select Journal Publications

### **2026**

Yiyun Luo, Xiaoxiao Duan, **Yanto Chandra** & Aiqi Wu (2026). Crowd-streaming by poverty-stricken entrepreneurs: Can poverty-as-comparison enhance poverty alleviation efforts?" ***International Journal of Information Management*** (Q1, IF: 27) <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijinfomgt.2026.103069" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** & Jianxiang Tan (2026). Re-Imagining the Epistemic Possibilities of GPT for Public Administration Research in Competitive Settings. ***Public Administration Review*** (Q1, IF: 4.9) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/400782921_Re-Imagining_the_Epistemic_Possibilities_of_GPT_for_Public_Administration_Research_in_Competitive_Settings" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/puar.70098" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Naikang Feng & **Yanto Chandra** (2026). Principal–Agent Issues When Governments Embrace AI Agents. ***Public Money & Management*** (Q2, IF: 2.1) <a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/09540962.2026.2642814" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Stratos Ramoglou, Liang Shang, **Yanto Chandra**, & Jeffery McMullen (2026). Are Language Models Intelligent Enough for Entrepreneurial Work? A Language-Centered Perspective. ***Journal of Business Venturing*** (FT50, Q1, IF: 8.7) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/400940672_Are_Language_Models_Intelligent_Enough_for_Entrepreneurial_Work_A_Language-Centered_Perspective" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0883902626000194" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Jianxiang Tan & **Yanto Chandra** (2026). Governing Military Artificial Intelligence Technologies: Manufacturing War or Peace with AI. ***Chinese Political Science Review*** (Q1, IF: 3.1) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/401362000_Governing_Military_Artificial_Intelligence_Technologies_Manufacturing_War_or_Peace_with_Artificial_Intelligence" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s41111-026-00334-5" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Naikang Feng & **Yanto Chandra** (2026). The Epistemic Impact of LLMs on Policymaking. ***Policy Studies Journal*** (Q1, IF: 4.9) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/399615634_The_Epistemic_Impact_of_Large_Language_Models_on_Policymaking" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/psj.70094" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Ziye Weng & **Yanto Chandra** (2026). Rumor Management during Natural Disasters: A “Whole of Government” Coordination Perspective. ***Government Information Quarterly***\*(Q1, IF: 10) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/399753355_Rumor_management_during_natural_disasters_A_whole_of_government_coordination_perspective" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.giq.2026.102104" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

### **2025**

Naikang Feng & **Yanto Chandra** (2025). Accountability in Government Use of AI: Citizen Concerns and Preferences. ***Public Administration*** (Q1, IF: 5.1) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/397060980_Accountability_in_Government_Use_of_AI_Citizen_Concerns_and_Preferences" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/padm.70030" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Stratos Ramoglou & **Yanto Chandra**, Qian Jin (2025). Opportunity Search in the Era of AI: Navigating Uncertainty in an Expanding Universe but Unknowable Futures. ***Journal of Management Studies*** 63(2): 695-721 (FT50, Q1, IF: 6.4) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/397060980_Accountability_in_Government_Use_of_AI_Citizen_Concerns_and_Preferences" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/joms.70011" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Jianxiang Tan & **Yanto Chandra** (2025). Artificial Intelligence as a Framework for Public Service Innovation. ***Public Money & Management*** 45(7): 785-787 (Q2, IF: 2.1)<a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/391975049_Debate_AI_as_a_framework_for_public_service_innovation" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/09540962.2025.2505206" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** & Ruiping Fan (2025). Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Human Relations: Eastern and Western Perspectives. ***Nature Springer*** <a href="" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** & Naikang Feng (2025). Algorithms for a New Season? Mapping a Decade of Research on the Artificial Intelligence-Driven Digital Transformation of Public Administration. ***Public Management Review*** (Q1, IF: 4.9) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/388148114_Algorithms_for_a_new_season_Mapping_a_decade_of_research_on_the_artificial_intelligence-driven_digital_transformation_of_public_administration" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/14719037.2025.2450680" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** & Simon Teasdale (2025). Social Enterprise as Political Work: Perspectives from the Global South.***Public Management Review*** 27(6): 1701-1740 (Q1, IF: 4.9). <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/385729286_Social_enterprise_as_political_work_perspectives_from_the_global_south" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/14719037.2024.2418025" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a> [Winner of 2025 Jenei Award, awarded by Public Management Review, Taylor & Francis, IRSPM 2026]{style="color:#ADD8E6; font-weight:bold;"}

### **2024**

Stratos Ramoglou, Reiner Schaefer, **Yanto Chandra**, & Jeff McMullen (2024). Artificial Intelligence Forces Us to Rethink Knightian Uncertainty: A Commentary on Townsend et al's "Are the Futures Computable. ***Academy of Management Review*** 50(2): 471-473. (Q1, IF: 10.5) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/380576424_Artificial_Intelligence_Forces_us_to_Rethink_Knightian_Uncertainty" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.5465/amr.2024.0127" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Richard Walker, Jiasheng Zheng, & **Yanto Chandra** (2024). Topics in Public Administration: Perspectives from Computational Social Sciences and Corpus Linguistics.***Cambridge Elements*** 5194: 834-855. (Q1, IF: 2.6) <a href="https://doi.org/10.1017/9781009378697" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

### **2023**

Richard Walker, Jiasheng Zhang, **Yanto Chandra**, Binzizi Dong, & Yara Wang (2023). Revisiting the Academic-Practitioner Divide: Evidence from Computational Social Sciences and Corpus Linguistics. ***Public Administration Review*** 83: 1599-1617. (Q1, IF: 4.9) <a href="https://repository.eduhk.hk/en/publications/revisiting-the-academicpractitioner-divide-evidence-from-computat/" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/puar.13724" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

### **2022**

**Yanto Chandra** & Qian Jin (2022). Winning the Heart and Shaping the Mind with “Serious Play”: The Efficacy of Social Entrepreneurship Comics as Ethical Business Pedagogy. ***Journal of Business Ethics*** 188: 441-465 (Q1, IF: 6.7) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/368546422_Winning_the_Heart_and_Shaping_the_Mind_with_'Serious_Play'_The_Efficacy_of_Social_Entrepreneurship_Comics_as_Ethical_Business_Pedagogy" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s10551-022-05283-y" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

### **2021**

**Yanto Chandra** & Arnil Paras (2021). Social Entrepreneurship in the Context of Disaster Recovery: Organizing for Public Value Creation. ***Public Management Review*** 23(12): 1856-1877. (Q1, IF: 4.9) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/342289445_Social_entrepreneurship_in_the_context_of_disaster_recovery_Organizing_for_public_value_creation" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/14719037.2020.1775282" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a> [Winner of the Kooiman Prize, awarded by Public Management Review, Taylor & Francis, IRSPM 2022]{style="color:#ADD8E6; font-weight:bold;"}

**Yanto Chandra**, Liang Shang, & Michael Roy (2021). Understanding Healthcare Social Enterprises: A New Public Governance Perspective.***Journal of Social Policy*** 5194: 834-855. (Q1, IF: 2.6) <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/348936829_Understanding_Healthcare_Social_Enterprises_A_New_Public_Governance_Perspective" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Preprint </a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1017/S0047279421000222" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a> [Best Paper Award, International Social Innovation Research Conference (ISIRC) 2020]{style="color:#ADD8E6; font-weight:bold;"}

### **2018**

**Yanto Chandra** (2018). New Narratives of Development Work? Making Sense of Social Entrepreneurs' Development Narratives Across Time and Economies. ***World Development*** 107: 306-326. (Q1, IF: 4.8) <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.worlddev.2018.02.033" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

### **2017**

**Yanto Chandra** (2017). Social Entrepreneurship as Emancipatory Work. ***Journal of Business Venturing*** 32(6): 657-673. (Q1, IF: 8.9) <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.worlddev.2018.02.033" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a> [<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K0el1pcgyQI"> <i class="bi bi-journal-bookmark"></i> YouTube </a>]{style="white-space: nowrap;"}

**Yanto Chandra** (2017). A Time-Based Process Model of International Entrepreneurial Opportunity Evaluation. ***Journal of International Business Studies*** 48: 423-451. (Q1, IF: 9.0) <a href="https://doi.org/10.1057/s41267-017-0068-x" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>


### Select Books and Chapters

Richard Walker, Jiasheng Zhang, & **Yanto Chandra** (2024). **Topics in Public Administration: Perspectives from Computational Social Sciences and Corpus Linguistics.** ***Cambridge University Press*** <a href="https://www.cambridge.org/core/elements/topics-in-public-administration/C9A9E7C04AF1DEAACB4EC7BBB9423DB5" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

Liang Shang & **Yanto Chandra** (2023). **Discrete Choice Experiments Using R: A How-To Guide for Social and Managerial Sciences.** ***Nature Springer*** <a href="https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-981-99-4562-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

 **Yanto Chandra**, & Liang Shang (2019). **Qualitative Research Using R: A Systematic Approach.** ***Nature Springer*** <a href="https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-981-13-3170-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>


### Creative Work

**Yanto Chandra** (2023). ***Comics Go to School***. A roadshow to bring comics to various campuses and schools as part of RAE for impact by CLASS CityUHK[<a href="https://comicsgoto.my.canva.site/comicsforimpact" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>] <a href="https://www.amazon.com/Mad-Spicy-Resto-Social-Entrepreneurship/dp/9577326080/ref=sr_1_2?crid=25VAG27A3REGB&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.k6lWN1nsApODs0nH76i8pSFimPgI3qzOgRFMSLuZTUg9MEuoqp5jLozFp3h6yT-sMLsKi3vF8vFS4yLGM3TtewVRlZBYuVfwd1cz3uxRmm4.5w0cqCv9xhIma6YY4BL5hKi7nUjGim8I6XGGh0GK46A&dib_tag=se&keywords=yanto+chandra&qid=1742896598&sprefix=cyanto+chandra%2Caps%2C325&sr=8-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener" style="white-space: nowrap; text-decoration: none;"> 🌟 [Mad Spicy Resto]{style="background-color: #e8f0fe; padding: 0.1rem 0.3rem; border-radius: 0.25rem;"} </a> <a href="https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/9577326099?ref_=dbs_m_mng_rwt_calw_tpbk_1&storeType=ebooks&qid=1740096795&sr=8-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener" style="white-space: nowrap; text-decoration: none;"> 🌟 [The O's Lab]{style="background-color: #e8f0fe; padding: 0.1rem 0.3rem; border-radius: 0.25rem;"} </a> <a href="https://www.amazon.com/Dragon-Tale-Story-Social-Entrepreneurship/dp/9577326072/ref=sr_1_1?crid=3MMBPRINQ4H9E&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.h0aKBxwKk-Gk0UAumruH5HTNup5GHq0Slo8Gx7SLgFQ.hnMeZIbJ5EBi93fqcrY1FzZWQgnXwDgnSHVVTwZAGu0&dib_tag=se&keywords=comic+yanto+chandra&qid=1742896565&sprefix=%2Caps%2C265&sr=8-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener" style="white-space: nowrap; text-decoration: none;"> 🌟 [Dragon Tale]{style="background-color: #e8f0fe; padding: 0.1rem 0.3rem; border-radius: 0.25rem;"} </a>

Varieties of field-based mini-documentaries, including: 
***Light Be: A Citizen Housing Project Turned into Govt's Transitional Housing Project*** (2018) <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bgM36f8wznQ" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-journal-bookmark"></i> YouTube </a>
***18Chefs: From Stigma to Opportunity*** (2016) <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J8kYurU4Jd4&t=1s" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-journal-bookmark"></i> YouTube </a>
***Dignity Kitchen: Disabled Cook, Elderly Eat, The Public Pay*** (2016) <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RK8SmgQK5bo" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-journal-bookmark"></i> YouTube </a>


### Op-Ed Columns

**Yanto Chandra** (2023). Can ChatGPT Do Data Science? ***AI Business*** 03/07/2023 <a href="https://aibusiness.com/nlp/can-chatgpt-do-data-science-" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** (2023). How to Keep Generative AI from Fully Replacing Humans: The Importance of Soft Skills and Entrepreneurship. ***South China Morning Post*** 08/10/2023 <a href="https://www.scmp.com/comment/opinion/article/3236783/how-keep-generative-ai-fully-replacing-humans-soft-skills-and-entrepreneurship" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** (2023). How Academia Can Embrace ChatGPT and Reignite the Love for Learning. ***South China Morning Post*** 22/03/2023 <a href="https://www.scmp.com/comment/opinion/article/3214311/how-academia-can-embrace-chatgpt-and-reignite-love-learning" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** (2023). How ChatGPT Will Change How We Search Online. ***South China Morning Post*** 02/03/2023 <a href="https://www.scmp.com/comment/opinion/article/3211809/move-over-google-chatgpt-and-its-will-change-how-we-search-online" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** (2022). What is the Public Responsibility of the Crypto Industry? ***Forkast*** 26/07/2022 <a href="https://forkast.news/what-is-crypto-industrys-public-responsibility/#google_vignette" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>

**Yanto Chandra** (2022). How NFTs Can Liberate Ordinary Artists. ***The Jakarta Post*** 19/04/2022 <a href="https://www.thejakartapost.com/paper/2022/04/19/how-non-fungible-tokens-liberate-ordinary-artists.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Published </a>
